name: Vietnamese Beef Pho (Soy-Free)
tagline: Charred-aromatic beef pho with rice noodles and herb plate

hero.alt: Bowl of pho with rare beef, rice noodles, and herb plate
hero.caption: phở bò — bone-broth deep

ingredients.name: Herb plate (basil, mint, cilantro, lime, chili)

steps.title: Blanch the bones
steps.detail: Cover bones with cold water, bring to a boil for 5 min, drain, rinse. Removes scum so the broth stays clear.
steps.tip: Skipping = murky pho. Worth the extra step.

steps.title: Char aromatics
steps.detail: Char halved onion and ginger directly on a flame or under a broiler until blackened. Toast spices in a dry pan 1 min.
steps.tip: Char until it looks ruined. That's where the smoke comes from.

steps.title: Long simmer
steps.detail: Combine bones, brisket, charred aromatics, spices, salt in a large pot with 4 quarts water. Simmer covered, very low, 2.5 hours, skimming foam. Pull brisket at 1.5 hr; slice when cool.
steps.tip: If it boils, you get cloudy broth. Bare bubbles only.

steps.title: Strain and assemble
steps.detail: Strain broth, return to clean pot. Stir in fish sauce. Cook noodles per package. Pile noodles in bowls with sliced brisket and raw eye of round; ladle hot broth over to cook the rare beef. Herb plate alongside.
steps.tip: Boiling broth poured over thin raw beef cooks it perfectly — pho's classic move.

healthFacts: Bone broth is collagen-rich — supportive of gut and joint health.
Char-roasting onion and ginger gives pho its signature smoke.
